content.thinking: The error is clear: `Could not resolve "./components/HomePage/home.jsx" from "src/App.jsx"`. This is a case sensitivity issue - Linux (Render) is case-sensitive while Windows is not. Let me check the actual file name and the import in App.jsx.

content.text: The build fails because Render runs Linux (case-sensitive filesystem) and it can't find `"./components/HomePage/home.jsx"`. Let me check the actual filename and the import.
Case mismatch. The file is `Home.jsx` (capital H) but the import says `home.jsx` (lowercase h). Windows doesn't care, but Linux does.

content.content: <tool_use_error>File has not been read yet. Read it first before writing to it.</tool_use_error>

toolUseResult: Error: File has not been read yet. Read it first before writing to it.

content.text: Let me also check the actual filenames for all the other lazy imports to catch any other case mismatches.
Good — `login.jsx` and `signup.jsx` are actually lowercase, so those imports are fine. Only the `Home.jsx` import has a case mismatch.

content.content: The file C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\App.jsx has been updated successfully.

content.text:
Fixed. The import was `home.jsx` (lowercase) but the actual file is `Home.jsx` (uppercase). Windows is case-insensitive so it worked locally, but Render runs Linux which is case-sensitive, causing the build failure.

Redeploy and it should build successfully.

message.content: it throws this error @client\src\components\HomePage\Canvas\CanvasEditor.jsx CanvasEditor.jsx:179  Unable to preventDefault inside passive event listener invocation.

content.thinking:
The user is getting the error "Unable to preventDefault inside passive event listener invocation" at line 179 of CanvasEditor.jsx.

Looking at line 179, that's inside `handleRectPointerDown` in the `CanvasMinimap` component:

```javascript
const handleRectPointerDown = (e) => {
    e.stopPropagation();
    e.preventDefault();  // line 179
```

The issue is that `onTouchStart` events are passive by default in modern browsers, and you can't call `preventDefault()` on passive event listeners. The `onTouchStart={handleRectPointerDown}` at line 228 is a React event handler, which in newer versions of React registers touch events as passive by default.


The fix is to check if the event is cancelable before calling `preventDefault()`, similar to how the `onMove` handler already does this at line 187. I should add a cancelable check to the initial `handleRectPointerDown` handler so it only prevents default behavior when the event allows it. React, `onTouchStart` is registered as a passive listener by default (since React 17+). So calling `e.preventDefault()` on a React `onTouchStart` handler will throw this warning.

The cleanest fix is to either:
1. Check `e.cancelable` before calling `preventDefault()`
2. Use a ref with `addEventListener('touchstart', handler, {passive: false})`

Option 1 is simpler but means `preventDefault` won't actually work (it just suppresses the warning). Option 2 properly prevents the default behavior.

For a minimap drag, we probably do want to prevent scrolling, so option 2 is better. But actually, looking at the code more carefully, the `e.preventDefault()` on line 179 is called on both mouse and touch events. For touch, it's passive by default.

I should check `cancelable` before calling `preventDefault`. The `onMove` handler already uses this pattern, and the document-level mousemove/touchmove listeners that handle the actual drag are set up with `{passive: false}`, so prevention is already covered there. Let me scan through for other `preventDefault` calls that might be in passive listeners—there are a few spots like line 179 in `handleRectPointerDown`, line 187 which is already guarded, and some in the Konva event handlers that I need to verify.

Since the error specifically points to line 179, I'll focus on fixing that one with a simple `cancelable` check.

content.text:
The error is on line 179 — `e.preventDefault()` inside `handleRectPointerDown`, which is called from `onTouchStart`. Modern browsers register touch events as passive by default, so `preventDefault()` isn't allowed.

The fix is to guard it with a `cancelable` check, matching the pattern already used on line 187:

content.content: The file C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\components\HomePage\Canvas\CanvasEditor.jsx has been updated successfully.

content.text: Fixed. React registers `onTouchStart` as a passive listener, which doesn't allow `preventDefault()`. Adding the `cancelable` check suppresses the error — the same pattern already used in the `onMove` handler at line 187.

content.thinking: The user wants to modify the MobileSidebarLink component so that when no user is logged in, the sign-out button says "Log in" instead of "Sign out", and when a user is logged in, it says "Sign out". Let me read the file first.
